description_long: This object resembles a horseshoe crab, featuring a broad, domed carapace that is olive-brown in color with subtle mottling. The front of the body is shield-like and smooth, while the underside reveals multiple jointed legs and a set of spiny appendages. A long, rigid tail spine (telson) extends straight from the rear, tapering to a point. The legs are arranged in a radial pattern beneath the carapace, with reddish-brown hues contrasting the main shell. The carapace is hard and slightly glossy, with a matte finish on the legs and tail. No visible claws or antennae are present, and the eyes are not clearly discernible.
description: A hard-shelled, olive-brown horseshoe crab with a domed carapace, spiny legs underneath, and a long pointed tail.
description_view_0: Frontal view showing the underside with multiple jointed legs and the base of the tail spine.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the domed carapace and the full length of the tail spine.
description_view_2: Dorsal (top) view displaying the smooth, broad carapace and the central tail spine.
description_view_3: Right side view similar to the left, showing the curvature of the shell and the tail.
